Repeat-proven 1,000-scout shadow layer

The scout layer is ready for guarded shadow production.

Two independent 1,000-scout runs turned live market slices into 5,878 information strings and 1,973 geometry cells. This page shows the full machine, then drills into one real packet so the system is legible from first principles.

What this isA repeat-proven first-layer market-sensing system: 1,000 narrow scouts per shadow run, with trade execution disabled.
What it saw265 live-market entities, 86 approved tools, 89 sources, plus node/Hydromancer surfaces.
What happened96/100 scouts completed in the latest run; 289 strings; 99 geometry cells; tournament said: scheduled shadow candidate.
00a / whole system map

Before the scout, here is the machine it belongs to.

The page starts with the whole system, then follows one real packet through the layer. Intake, tools, prompt lab, scout swarm, graph memory, attention gate, geometry, verifier/tool builders, and the user surface are shown together so the run feels like a designed machine, not a pile of JSON.

01 | Touched in this run

Signal Intake

The front door. Posts, event feeds, node data, wallet traces, charts, filings, calendars, and future mempool feeds arrive here as untrusted clues.

02 | Touched in this run

Tool Atlas

The approved instrument wall. Scouts can draw from any read-only tool or source with schemas, costs, permissions, and logs.

03 | Touched in this run

Prompt Lab

The quality-control room. Prompts must prove they create useful strings before thousands of cheap calls are allowed.

04 | Touched in this run

Scout Swarm

The wide sensing layer. Each scout studies one tiny slice of the market and explains the causal chain it sees.

05 | Touched in this run

Information Map

The memory. Claims become queryable objects with source refs, time horizons, quality flags, and links to related claims.

06 | Touched in this run

Attention Gate

The triage desk. It compares many strings, finds overlaps and contradictions, and chooses what deserves deeper work.

07 | Touched in this run

Alpha Geometry + Evolution

The shape layer. Strings become market cells with coordinates, route directives, evaluator scores, mutations, and A/B experiment plans.

08 | Touched in this run

Verifier + Tool Builders

The due-diligence room and workshop. Strong claims get checked; missing capabilities become new tools.

09 | Touched in this run

Monitor + PM

The user surface. It turns the machinery into a calm explanation, alert, or portfolio-aware decision path.

Signal Intake

The front door. Posts, event feeds, node data, wallet traces, charts, filings, calendars, and future mempool feeds arrive here as untrusted clues.

ConsumesExternal and node-native data
EmitsTimestamped evidence refs

Why it exists: Never let a screenshot become truth. Resolve it into source-linked evidence first.

Tool Atlas

The approved instrument wall. Scouts can draw from any read-only tool or source with schemas, costs, permissions, and logs.

ConsumesTool candidates and source needs
EmitsBudgeted tool/source calls and logs

Why it exists: Wide scouts only work if they can reach the whole approved estate without spraying random calls.

Prompt Lab

The quality-control room. Prompts must prove they create useful strings before thousands of cheap calls are allowed.

ConsumesPrompt contract and sample outputs
EmitsPassed or blocked scale gate

Why it exists: DeepSeek Flash gets scaled only after the prompt proves it returns usable intelligence.

Scout Swarm

The wide sensing layer. Each scout studies one tiny slice of the market and explains the causal chain it sees.

ConsumesEntity, horizon, lens, evidence
EmitsHypothesis and strings

Why it exists: Thousands of cheap scouts map the market without pretending every observation is actionable.

Information Map

The memory. Claims become queryable objects with source refs, time horizons, quality flags, and links to related claims.

ConsumesScout outputs
EmitsQueryable intelligence graph

Why it exists: The map becomes memory and routing infrastructure, not a sidecar notebook.

Attention Gate

The triage desk. It compares many strings, finds overlaps and contradictions, and chooses what deserves deeper work.

ConsumesMany information strings
EmitsPromoted hypotheses

Why it exists: This is the anti-spam layer between broad sensing and expensive verification.

Alpha Geometry + Evolution

The shape layer. Strings become market cells with coordinates, route directives, evaluator scores, mutations, and A/B experiment plans.

ConsumesInformation strings, tool receipts, source families
EmitsMap shape, routing directive, evolved research policy

Why it exists: The desk should improve its own prompting, slicing, and tool use from measured outcomes.

Verifier + Tool Builders

The due-diligence room and workshop. Strong claims get checked; missing capabilities become new tools.

ConsumesPromoted claims and gaps
EmitsValidated claims or new tools

Why it exists: Agents should improve the desk itself, especially around node discovery and mempool intelligence.

Monitor + PM

The user surface. It turns the machinery into a calm explanation, alert, or portfolio-aware decision path.

ConsumesStored graph and verifier outputs
EmitsHuman-readable product surface

Why it exists: This is where the machine becomes legible enough to trust from a phone.

00 / neural-net shape

Talis is a research network, not one smart prompt.

Think of the desk as a market-native neural system: sensors feed narrow receptive fields; scout outputs become activations; graph memory stores the latent state; attention promotes the important clusters; geometry routes spend; reward loops evolve prompts, tools, and routing. This page shows that shape using the real run packet.

16/16system layers passed
canonical trace artifacttrace source
4allowed tools
5/18data surface coverage
Sensory organs

2 receipts

Event feed, Hydromancer, our node, market state, and future mempool streams enter as source-linked observations.

Receptive field

DYDX / 1w / sentiment

Each scout sees one market cell so thousands of calls can tile the market instead of duplicating attention.

Activation

4 strings

The model output becomes causal strings with mechanism, expected outcome, kill signal, scores, and evidence refs.

Memory

10 surfaces

The run writes separate objects for hypotheses, strings, evidence edges, graph nodes, node intelligence, tools, geometry, and evolution.

Attention

isyn_889ee5b95006

Synthesis promotes only cross-string confluence or tension into expensive verifier/specialist budget.

Latent geometry

widen scouts

The map shape becomes a routing instruction: verify, widen scouts, widen sources, repair sources, or resolve tension.

Reward signal

75%

MarketEvolve scores the research policy itself, then creates mutations and hard experiments instead of blindly trusting vibes.

Tool creation

exploit learned tool surface

Missing edges become tool proposals; useful tools are evaluated, promoted, and returned to the scout harness.

00b / recurrent loop

The system learns by changing the harness around the model.

The model is one organ. The intelligence comes from the loop: tool receipts constrain the scout, map memory changes the next prompt, geometry changes the next slice, evaluator scores mutate the policy, and successful tools become new senses.

00_schema

Schema Migrator

all downstream persistence

01_prompt_gate

Prompt Lab

scout scale-up controller

02_seed_slice

Seed Router

tool atlas and scout harness

03_tool_harness

Tool Atlas + Harness

model prompt and source provenance graph

04_model_call

Deep Scout Model

normalizer and persistor

05_persistence

Normalizer + Persistor

monitor, synthesis, geometry, verifier

06_tool_creation

Analysis Tool Creator

learned runtime and future scout atlas retrieval

07_attention

Information Synthesis Gate

verifier and specialist budget router

Shape to next action

This is the recurrence we care about: the map is read by a native tool, the route changes, and a seed or work order is emitted for the next pass.

Shape Observed

widen_scouts

The alpha-geometry field was read as a routing object.

Tool Called

tic://tool/talis_native/plan_alpha_geometry_actions@v1

log=derived_from_live_scout_canary_report

Cortex Decides

Route Selected

widen_scouts

Work Emitted

Scout Executes

pending_next_scout

live_100_shape_emitted, needs_independent_replication

The emitted seed now carries the same contract the cortex sees: which edge is missing, who owns it, which tools should run after the shape reader, and what would count as success.

{
  "action": null,
  "cell_key": "DYDX|1w|sentiment|calendar_catalyst",
  "missing_edges": null,
  "next_tools": [
    "tic://tool/builtin/query_events_recent@v1",
    "tic://tool/builtin/query_timeseries@v1"
  ],
  "owner": null,
  "seed_id": "seed_live_100_shape_followup_DYDX_1w_sentiment_calendar_catalyst",
  "shape_reader_first": "tic://tool/talis_native/plan_alpha_geometry_actions@v1",
  "success_gate": null
}

The routed scout is then graded against the geometry contract. This is the closed loop: the shape directs work, the scout must move the missing edge, and the evaluator tells the cortex whether the route succeeded.

{
  "quality_flags": [
    "live_100_shape_emitted",
    "needs_independent_replication"
  ],
  "status": "pending_next_scout",
  "success_gate": null
}

shape_route_rules:
  - This seed exists because the 100-scout live map requested follow-up work.
  - Use the native shape-reader evidence and allowed tools to satisfy or falsify the success_gate.
  - Your information_strings should explicitly change, confirm, contradict, or kill the missing_edges.

Current activation

DYDX sentiment will remain neutral-to-negative over the next week as the funding spread anomaly (8.26% annualized) signals persistent short bias, despite the platform upgrade catalyst being already priced into a 7% pump that lacks volume confirmation.

00c / market map generation

The full map is a lattice plus a coverage ledger.

The desk does not know the market is covered because it launched 1,000 scouts. It knows coverage by comparing the generated seed lattice against logged cells, freshness, source receipts, geometry routes, and missing surfaces. A cycle is a strategic sweep; the map is complete only when the ledger proves no important blind zone is stale or unexplained.

entities

265

The instrument universe. Today it is the default desk universe unless the run scope overrides it.

horizons

6

Intraday through structural time windows; the horizon decides freshness and kill-signal expectations.

lenses

15

Macro, microstructure, options, sentiment, rotation, on-chain, catalyst, anomaly, and other evidence lanes.

bias modes

6

Contrarian, consensus, frontier, tail-risk, mean-reversion, and momentum postures.

valid cells

132048

The theoretical grid after asset-class validity removes nonsensical combinations.

covered cells

1

Cells currently proven touched in coverage_cells within this smoke database.

missing cells

132047

Deterministic gap count: valid cells minus covered cells, before intentional exclusions.

source universe

18

Known source surfaces: node, Hydromancer, web, filings, macro, options, prediction markets, mempool, social, and experimental priors.

current cell

HYPE / intraday / on_chain / frontier / validator_unstake

This page follows one specimen cell from that larger market lattice.

split policy

How 1,000 scouts are allocated.

Latin hypercube over valid entity x horizon x lens x bias cells. Themes reserve budget, then the remaining scouts spread across the valid grid. Coverage penalties reduce recently covered dead zones; topology and alpha-geometry pull scouts toward frontiers, tensions, source repair, and verifier-ready cells; calendar gates prepend must-research events.

coverage penalty

recently covered cells with no downstream publish are downweighted

coverage_log

topology frontier boost

sparse/frontier regions are oversampled, dense consensus regions are underweighted

topology_density_map

alpha-geometry route seeds

verify, repair, resolve-tension, widen-source, and widen-scout cells get explicit next-cycle scouts

information_geometry_snapshots from the prior cycle

calendar gate

must-research event cells are prepended before the normal random budget

calendar_gate / catalyst clock

BM25 tool retrieval

each seed receives lexical candidates plus dynamic tool/source matches for its query text

tool_atlas

What would prove the map is full?

One 1,000-scout cycle is strategic coverage, not mathematical proof that every market state was exhausted.

Current deterministic coverage ratio in this smoke run: 0.0000. That low number is expected for a one-cell smoke; a production 1,000-scout run should move this ledger, not merely print successful calls.

coverage proof

01

Every valid cell has a coverage_log row with freshness and outcome state.

coverage proof

02

Every active theme has dedicated cells across affected entities and horizons.

coverage proof

03

Every geometry route directive is either consumed by a later seed or explicitly expired.

coverage proof

04

Every high-value data surface has at least one live receipt path or a tool proposal explaining the gap.

coverage proof

05

Coverage dashboards show blind zones, stale zones, crowded zones, and frontier zones separately.

seed_manifest.json for generated cellscoverage_log tabletool_atlas retrieval receiptsinformation_geometry_snapshotssystem_trace.json

This is the known source taxonomy, not every possible source on earth. Unknown or missing surfaces must become tool proposals with explicit gaps.

00d / market-map governor

The gap ledger becomes an allocation brain.

The coverage audit is deterministic math. The governor sits above it and ranks the frontier: which missing cells deserve the next scouts, which source surfaces those scouts should touch, and what compact context a model can use to propose repair work without pretending the whole market is known.

265known entities
132048valid cells
131949missing cells
high_frontier_pressure_low_absolute_coveragecompletion pressure
ready_from_live_100geometry field
readyshape action plan
no gaps

No ranked gaps exported.

Run the governor after the coverage audit to see scout allocation priorities.

cortex + geometry

The shape tells the cortex where to move next.

The market map is not just a checklist. Alpha geometry turns strings into coordinates: source independence, frontier pressure, tension, fragility, support, and verifier readiness. The action planner translates that field into concrete next moves: verify, repair sources, resolve tension, widen sources, or replicate with independent scouts.

Native self-inspection tool

Geometry-routed and cortex-routed scouts now receive tic://tool/talis_native/plan_alpha_geometry_actions@v1 in their harness. That means an agent can ask the desk itself: “what does the current shape say I should do next?” before spending more external calls.

Shape tools requested by the field

These are the tools the geometry thinks would move a coordinate or close a missing edge.

[
  {
    "tool_uri": "tic://tool/builtin/query_events_recent@v1",
    "why": "suggested by top geometry actions"

  {
    "tool_uri": "tic://tool/builtin/query_timeseries@v1",
    "why": "suggested by top geometry actions"
  }
]
allocation

How the next 1,000 agents get sent out.

Budget lanes group the ranked gaps by asset class, horizon, and lens. This keeps broad coverage while still over-weighting live tradeable frontiers, node/Hydromancer gaps, fresh horizons, and missing source edges.

Source surfaces the governor wants to improve

These are not vague research wishes. Each surface needs typed receipts, timestamps, raw artifact refs, and evidence IDs before it can become map memory.

Seed-cell preview

The governor emits concrete seed packets that can be handed to the scout harness.

[]

LLM governor context

A frontier model may improve the plan, but only inside this context and only if deterministic promotion gates accept the output. In this run: enabled=False; promoted seeds=0; assessment=frontier gaps remain large; allocate next scouts to hyperliquid_perp intraday/on_chain/microstructure/smart_money lanes.

Live 100 audit used deterministic governor projection; frontier LLM governor remains a next scheduled enhancement.
00e / self-healing map

The map assigns workers to repair itself.

Coverage gaps should become action, not dashboard guilt. The self-healing planner reads the canonical trace, posts durable repair contracts, then a worker claims those contracts and either reads the map shape through the tool harness or turns missing surfaces into concrete tool proposals. An LLM can improve the plan from a compact context packet, while deterministic rails keep every order tied to a missing edge and a success gate.

The orders now cross the execution boundary: each one becomes a schedulable task contract with allowed tools, source refs, a success gate, a stop condition, and idempotent repost behavior.

Repair worker executed

The posted contracts are not decorative. The worker claims the task, starts it, dispatches approved read-only tools when the task is a route, creates analysis-tool proposals when the task is a missing surface, then completes or fails the contract with a proof payload.

claimed6
completed6
failed0
tool proposals8
promotion reports0
observations6

Worker context packet

This is the compact state a repair worker receives before it acts.

cyclecycle_live_scout_canary_20260522T141613Z
routewiden_scouts
info stringsnone
evidence4
market universe{"entity_count": 265, "source_quality": "live"}
data universe{"skills": 0, "sources": 89, "tools": 86}

LLM repair-planner prompt

This is where a cheap or strong model can propose extra repair orders. The output still has to be strict JSON and pass deterministic gates before a worker is allowed to spend tools.

Use the live 100-scout audit to emit only repair orders that move a geometry coordinate, close a missing edge, or create a source/tool proposal with an eval gate.
00f / 100-scout scale gate

Before spending on 1,000 scouts, the desk ran a 100-scout rehearsal.

This is a deterministic readiness slice: real seed generation, real scout harness, real storage, real information map, real geometry, real governor, and real self-healing worker, with the external provider layer replaced by an offline model/tool shim. It proves the machine can carry 100 packets through the system before we buy live model calls.

unknownreadiness status
96/100scouts completed
288information strings
24shape-routed tasks
0tool-loop revisions
6repair tasks completed

What the pilot proved

Every scout produced usable map memory, the duplicate rate stayed at 3% and evidence calls were clean. The shape layer then routed the strongest single-scout cells to independent replication instead of pretending one scout is enough support.

The map screamed this next

The highest-priority shape actions are not trade orders; they are the next research move the map demands before conviction can rise.

seed_router / widen_scouts

replicate_with_independent_scouts

V|1d|sentiment|

Gate: second scout confirms, contradicts, or kills the string

Missing: frontier_cell -> next_receptive_field, single_scout_cell -> independent_replication

seed_router / widen_scouts

replicate_with_independent_scouts

INIT|intraday|money_velocity|

Gate: second scout confirms, contradicts, or kills the string

Missing: frontier_cell -> next_receptive_field, single_scout_cell -> independent_replication

seed_router / widen_scouts

replicate_with_independent_scouts

ZEREBRO|intraday|on_chain|

Gate: second scout confirms, contradicts, or kills the string

Missing: frontier_cell -> next_receptive_field, single_scout_cell -> independent_replication

What the next 100 should cover

The coverage governor still sees the market as huge: this run covered 100 cells out of 132048. The next allocation is concentrated where the frontier gaps are densest.

18 scouts

hyperliquid_perp / intraday / on_chain

Top unresolved hyperliquid_perp gaps concentrate in intraday/on_chain.

18 scouts

hyperliquid_perp / intraday / microstructure

Top unresolved hyperliquid_perp gaps concentrate in intraday/microstructure.

18 scouts

hyperliquid_perp / intraday / smart_money

Top unresolved hyperliquid_perp gaps concentrate in intraday/smart_money.

Honest boundary

This proves orchestration, not live provider quality.

Next:

00g / 100-scout live ramp

The live provider layer is proving distribution quality.

This gate calls the real provider across a broader seed distribution under a hard cap, records the exact prompts and responses, and refuses to open the 1,000-scout ramp until provider quality, evidence, storage, and geometry stay clean.

passlive canary status
96/100scouts completed
$0.36596estimated model/tool spend
289live strings stored
100provider calls captured
99geometry cells

Provider and data preflight

The canary checks the live model import, the local TIC substrate, the approved tool atlas, and the market universe before a single model token is bought.

yestic root
yesprovider import
yestool atlas
yesmarket universe

00g.1 / 100-scout proof

The 100-scout slice produced real map memory, not just pretty output.

This chapter is the run audit: provider behavior, seed coverage, storage counts, graph shape, self-healing work, and the honest blemishes that should be watched before the next full shadow cycle.

96/100scouts completed
289information strings
99geometry cells
24routing tasks
$0.36596estimated spend
yesshadow-ready
notrade execution

Provider health

DeepSeek handled 98 calls; fallback handled 2. The important watch item is not cost, it is whether timeout and JSON failure rates stay bounded as cadence rises.

100provider calls
2primary fallbacks
17.4494savg latency
22.856sp95 latency
98.773smax latency

Seed slice

The router did not spray randomly. It split the 100 scouts across asset class, horizon, lens, and bias, with unique-cell ratio 1.0.

{
  "asset_classes": {
    "equity": 8,
    "hyperliquid_perp": 88,
    "index_macro": 4
  },
  "horizons": {
    "1d": 17,
    "1m": 17,
    "1q": 17,
    "1w": 19,
    "intraday": 15,
    "structural": 15
  },
  "lenses": {
    "anomaly": 6,
    "catalyst": 7,
    "factor": 6,
    "filing": 1,
    "macro": 6,
    "microstructure": 6,
    "money_velocity": 6,
    "on_chain": 6,
    "options_flow": 8,
    "polymarket": 10,
    "rotation": 10,
    "sentiment": 10,
    "smart_money": 4,
    "structural": 7,
    "vol_surface": 7
  },
  "unique_cell_count": 100
}

Storage proof

This is the “where did it go?” answer. Scout output is not a dead transcript; it became graph rows, evidence joins, geometry snapshots, tool proposals, and repair tasks.

96

hypotheses

Top-level scout claims that can be verified or killed.

289

information strings

Typed causal strings stored as map memory.

508

map nodes

Graph objects created from strings, evidence, and actors.

1286

map edges

Queryable links between entities, mechanisms, evidence, and routes.

321

tool calls

Read-only receipts available for replay and citation.

357

node observations

Node-derived observations available to scouts.

174

tool proposals

Missing tools the system proposed from its own gaps.

6

task contracts

Self-healing work orders posted from the map shape.

What the geometry asked for next

The highest-scoring cells still ask for independent replication or source expansion. That is exactly the right posture for frontier claims: the shape can scream, but the verifier still has to earn the trade.

widen_scouts

DYDX|1w|sentiment|calendar_catalyst

Tools the system wants to create

Tool creation is now part of analysis. When the map sees missing node or source edges, it can propose concrete readers with promotion gates.

Honest blemishes

watch

1 scout error(s) need inspection.

This does not block shadow scale, but it should be tracked against the next full run.

watch

2 primary provider timeout(s) used fallback.

This does not block shadow scale, but it should be tracked against the next full run.

watch

3 scouts missed the hypothesis field.

This does not block shadow scale, but it should be tracked against the next full run.

watch

3 scouts missed information strings.

This does not block shadow scale, but it should be tracked against the next full run.

00h / live tournament gate

The system now grades which live ramp it has earned.

This is the evaluator-guided evolution step: live reports become candidates, candidates are scored on reliability, latency, string yield, evidence support, duplicate rate, prompt structure, geometry impact, and self-healing. A wider live run is allowed only when the candidate wins every hard gate.

promote_to_scheduled_production_candidatepromotion decision
yesready for 100 scouts
yesready for 1,000 scouts
yesscheduled production
0.982best success rate
0provider error rate
989geometry cells

Honest readiness call

Two independent 1,000-scout shadow runs passed scale gates under the same prompt/provider policy, and their repeatability metrics stayed inside the production stability band. The system may move to a scheduled shadow-production candidate posture with hard caps, audit capture, and no trade execution.

The live policy has now passed two independent 1,000-scout shadow runs with stable quality, geometry, prompt structure, duplicate rate, and information-string yield. It is ready for a guarded scheduled shadow production job, with trade execution still disabled.

Next evolution arms

The next paid call is the guarded scheduled shadow-production job below: hard caps, full audit capture, and no trade execution.

schedule_guarded_shadow_production

Move from one-off canaries to a scheduled shadow-production job while keeping hard spend caps, full artifact capture, and no trade execution.

A scheduled job remains shadow-only unless daily tournament reports keep provider errors, duplicate rate, structural flags, geometry cells, and information-string yield inside the proven repeatability envelope.

00i / guarded shadow job

The promotion now has a production-shaped harness.

The guarded shadow job ran and its post-run gates passed. The key distinction: this is allowed to sense, store, score, and route. It is not allowed to place trades.

allowed

forbidden

exchange_order_submission, wallet_or_key_mutation, position_sizing_commit, portfolio_rebalance_commit

Exact scheduled command

The scheduler should run this only after the tournament report remains current. Add --allow-live-spend only when deliberately opening the model-call valve.

Swipe walkthrough

Read this like a deck.

Swipe card by card. Each card says what went in, what came out, and which real receipt proves it happened.

Swipe sideways
Start1

A market cell is born.

Input: market universe, horizon grid, lens rules, themes, and coverage state. Output: one typed research cell that says exactly what this scout is allowed to care about.

Input: stored strings and their source families. Output: market cells with coordinates, scores, and a route directive that tells the next layer what to do.

The system edits its future behavior.

Input: cycle metrics, geometry, tool usage, and learned-tool outcomes. Output: an evaluator score, a candidate mutation, and a hard experiment plan.

mutationexploit_learned_tool_surface
whyLearned tools are producing successful evidence calls; bias future scout tool surfaces toward them.
data coverage18 surfaces tracked
Arena9

Candidate policies must earn promotion.

Input: one evaluation plus the bounded population policy. Output: candidate children with proof contracts, kill signals, population gates, and matched A/B evidence before any child can become active.

candidate children1
frontier queue3
latest verdictpromote_candidate
01 / why this layer exists

The market is too wide for one prompt.

Layer 1 solves that by running many narrow scouts. Each scout gets one receptive field, a small tool budget, and a strict output contract. The goal is not instant genius; the goal is high-quality coverage that compounds into a shared map.

1

Cut the market into cells

Each scout receives one entity, horizon, lens, bias, theme, and clock.

2

Fetch evidence before reasoning

The desk scores the full approved atlas, selects a tiny call budget, and attaches compact receipts.

3

Prompt against a contract

The model must return JSON with thesis, mechanism, kill signal, scores, and refs.

4

Validate the response

Weak strings, invented tools, missing refs, and uncalibrated scores are waste signals.

5

Route to memory

Every useful finding enters the information map as evidence, strings, nodes, edges, and missing-edge prompts.

The reusable field of view

Every scout is framed by the same six fields. That makes thousands of cheap calls comparable, replayable, and measurable.

Entitythe object being sensed
Horizonthe expiry window
Lensthe allowed data universe
Biasthe scout posture
Themethe causal pattern
Clockthe freshness boundary
Layer 1 is allowed to say: "inside this cell, these receipts imply this conditional string."
Layer 1 is not allowed to say: "I generally feel bullish or bearish."
02 / the real input packet

Here is exactly what this scout was handed.

The scout did not receive a vague request to be smart. It received a precise market cell, a clock, a scored subset of approved tools, and evidence receipts that can be audited later.

Assigned cell

This is the scout's world for the call.

EntityDYDX
Horizon1w
Lenssentiment
Biasconsensus_confirm
Themecalendar_catalyst
Clock2026-05-22T14:17:49.166795+00:00

Selected atlas slice

The scout can draw from the approved read-only atlas. This call exposes the relevant subset and keeps spend under a hard cap.

tic://tool/builtin/query_events_recent@v1tic://tool/parallel/parallel_search@v1tic://tool/builtin/query_timeseries@v1tic://tool/builtin/query_source_health@v1tic://tool/agent_native/find_confluence@v1tic://tool/builtin/query_recent_news@v1tic://tool/agent_native/semantic_search@v1tic://tool/builtin/get_geopolitical_tone@v1tic://tool/builtin/get_hl_candles@v1tic://tool/builtin/synthesize_confluence_window@v1tic://tool/perplexity/web_search@v1tic://tool/builtin/query_claims_by_entity@v1
03 / the bigger data substrate

The scout sees a selected slice of a much larger estate.

This proof run touched only part of the data layer. That is intentional: the harness exposes the most relevant sources first, records what was touched, and names the important surfaces still missing. More data only helps when it closes a map edge.

5/18data surfaces touched by this run
5active receipt links
3typed edges formed
39%substrate coverage score
Touched

Event feed

What happened. Did a fresh event enter the market clock?

Receipts: tc_0ba609789e0044bf8baf347d

Touched

Market state

Can it matter. What do depth, open interest, funding, and tape context say?

Receipts: tc_0ba609789e0044bf8baf347d

Touched

Parallel and web search

What the open web knows. Do live web/Parallel sources add primary evidence, consensus context, or contradictions?

Receipts: tc_7db59320cd1b4d4ab2960291

Touched

Options, vol, and derivatives

How risk is priced. Do IV, skew, funding, OI, liquidations, or basis confirm/crowd the claim?

Receipts: tc_0ba609789e0044bf8baf347d

Touched

Official macro

What the regime allows. Do FRED, Fed, Treasury, EIA, DOL, BEA, or survey feeds change the regime backdrop?

Receipts: tc_7db59320cd1b4d4ab2960291

available

Hydromancer actor graph

Who is involved. Are the actors skilled, noisy, crowded, or historically predictive?

available

Our HL node

What our node knows. Do our own rejects, fills, and state deltas show healthy or stressed flow?

available

Builder flow

Where orders route. Which builders, users, and coins are carrying the flow?

available

Wallet route state

Where supply goes. Does the actor restake, bridge, idle, route to CEX, or hit sellable liquidity?

04 / connection expansion

The next call is chosen by the missing connection.

The system should not spend calls just to feel busy. It should ask: which missing edge would change the map? Actor route, builder flow, source health, pending intent, order-flow expression. If the edge needs a tool we do not have, the gap becomes a tool proposal.

event -> DYDX market_stateformed by this run
event -> vol_positioningformed by this run
macro_regime -> market_state_constraintformed by this run
high priority

Resolve actor route state

The scout knows a DYDX event exists, but not where the actor routes after the event.

Edge: event -> wallet_route -> sellable_liquidity

tic://tool/learned/wallet_route_state_monitor@v1 (needs atlas/proposal)
medium priority

Join builder flow

Builder fills can reveal whether the flow is concentrated, informed, or mechanically routed.

Edge: wallet -> builder -> fills

tic://tool/hydromancer/get_builder_fills@v1 (needs atlas/proposal)
high priority

Resolve source health and citations

A high-quality string should know whether every receipt is fresh and addressable.

Edge: claim -> citation -> source_health

tic://tool/builtin/query_source_health@v1tic://tool/learned/evidence_ref_resolver@v1 (needs atlas/proposal)
medium priority

Compare event-market odds

Prediction markets can disagree with headlines and reveal where catalyst probability is mispriced.

Edge: event_market_probability -> catalyst_disagreement

tic://source/polymarket/polymarket_search (needs atlas/proposal)tic://source/hl/hl_outcomes (needs atlas/proposal)
05 / what the scout sees

The prompt is the model's cockpit.

The user prompt carries the cell, clock, evidence, prior strings, and allowed tools. The system prompt carries the contract. Together they make the model operate like an instrument instead of an open-ended chat.

Real prompt excerpt

This is the scout's actual input surface: timestamp, cell, evidence, and permitted tools.

as_of_utc=2026-05-22T14:17:49.166795+00:00

  horizon=1w
  lens=sentiment
  bias_mode=consensus_confirm
  theme=calendar_catalyst

Prioritize primary sources, current data, and contradictions.", "results": [{"source": null, "text": "\u2022 DYDX currently trading at $0.61 (+1.75% in 24h) \u2022 RSI neutral at 46.94 while MACD signals bearish momentum \u2022 <strong>Major platform upgrade announcement</strong> drove 7% price surge this week \u00b7 The primary catalyst behind

Narrow receptive field

The scout gets one cell, not the whole market.

DYDX / 1w / sentiment / calendar_catalyst

Receipts before opinion

Tool output is attached before the model writes.

2 real evidence packets were included in the user prompt.

Budgeted atlas slice

The scout can use the approved read-only atlas, then suggest only the relevant exposed URIs.

12 selected tool/source URIs were exposed.

Structured output

The answer must become machine memory.

hypothesis, rationale, suggested_tools, information_strings, evidence_refs, kill_signal

06 / real receipts

The scout reasons from receipts, not vibes.

These are the four receipts in this run: an event-style feed, Hydromancer actor context, our node state, and market time series. The mix matters because node intelligence is strongest when actor quality, source state, and market plumbing meet.

tc_0ba609789e0044bf8baf347d

Event feed

DYDX: funding spread 8.26% — short HlPerp@+10.95% / long BybitPerp@+2.69% (real basis 2.39%, HL cap: True) Event time 2026-05-20T05:31:09.520215+00:00; label unknown; depth ratio unknown.

tc_7db59320cd1b4d4ab2960291

parallel_search@v1

{"as_of": "2026-05-22T14:17:49.163359+00:00", "n_results": 10, "objective": "Find fresh, source-diverse evidence for DYDX calendar_catalyst sentiment 1w. Prioritize primary sour...

07 / how it draws the conclusion

The conclusion is shown as an audit ladder.

We are not exposing hidden model thought. We are exposing the structure the system can audit: input fact, allowed transformation, emitted conditional string, and kill signal.

Observed input

The cell says what counts

DYDX is the object. 1w is the window. sentiment is the evidence lane.

then
System transformation

The scout is prevented from drifting into broad market commentary.

Observed input

The receipts say what happened

DYDX: funding spread 8.26% — short HlPerp@+10.95% / long BybitPerp@+2.69% (real basis 2.39%, HL cap: True) Event time 2026-05-20T05:31:09.520215+00:00; label unknown; depth ratio unknown.

then
System transformation

This becomes only a candidate observation until the rest of the evidence can support or weaken it.

Observed input

The market plumbing says whether it can matter

Market state gives depth and positioning context.

then
System transformation

The scout must connect source fact to liquidity, route, positioning, and horizon.

Observed input

Node and actor quality say what to inspect next

Hydromancer and node evidence add actor and execution-quality context.

then
System transformation

The output becomes a verifier-ready conditional claim, not a final decision.

08 / what came out

The output is a memory object, not a final answer.

The scout emits a conditional claim plus information strings. Those objects carry mechanism, expected outcome, kill signal, evidence refs, scores, and quality flags so the rest of Talis can compare, verify, expire, or promote them.

Emitted hypothesis

DYDX sentiment will remain neutral-to-negative over the next week as the funding spread anomaly (8.26% annualized) signals persistent short bias, despite the platform upgrade catalyst being already priced into a 7% pump that lacks volume confirmation.

Persistent short funding cost suggests market participants are willing to pay to hold shorts, implying bearish conviction that overrides transient upgrade news.

Verifier handoff

Funding spread remains above 5% annualized 3 days from now, or price fails to hold $0.61.

Kill signal: Funding spread drops below 3% annualized and price breaks above $0.66 on increasing volume, contradicting short-bias thesis.

55%confidence
50%conviction
60%novelty
30%crowdedness

Actual structured fragment

This is the machine-readable surface the map can store and the next agents can inspect.

{
  "hypothesis": "DYDX sentiment will remain neutral-to-negative over the next week as the funding spread anomaly (8.26% annualized) signals persistent short bias, despite the platform upgrade catalyst being already priced into a 7% pump that lacks volume confirmation.",
  "confidence": 0.55,
  "rationale_brief": "Funding rate anomaly contradicts bullish upgrade narrative; RSI neutral at 46.94, MACD bearish. No fresh catalyst to sustain momentum. Thin evidence requires kill_signal tracking.",
  "first_information_string": {
    "title": "Funding spread anomaly signals persistent short bias",
    "thesis": "DYDX funding spread of 8.26% annualized, with HL perp capped, indicates structurally elevated short demand that should cap any upside from narrative catalysts.",
    "mechanism": "Persistent short funding cost suggests market participants are willing to pay to hold shorts, implying bearish conviction that overrides transient upgrade news.",
    "expected_outcome": "Funding spread remains above 5% annualized 3 days from now, or price fails to hold $0.61.",
    "kill_signal": "Funding spread drops below 3% annualized and price breaks above $0.66 on increasing volume, contradicting short-bias thesis.",
    "evidence_refs": [
      "tc_0ba609789e0044bf8baf347d"
    ]
  }
}
09 / how it fits the full system

The first layer becomes the routing substrate.

After Layer 1, the claim is no longer trapped in a transcript. Tool receipts, source refs, strings, coverage gaps, nodes, and edges land in the information map. Synthesis compares them; verifiers attack them; tool-builder agents repair missing capabilities.

Information map

Stores strings with source refs, time windows, quality flags, entity chains, and map links.

Attention gate

Finds confluences and contradictions before expensive verification spend.

Verifier agents

Receive the hypothesis, evidence refs, expected outcome, and kill signal.

Tool builders

When analysis is missing, agents propose, test, and promote better node-discovery tools.

10 / exact packet trace

Every party, every handoff, every persisted object.

This is the step-by-step system trace for the exported run. The left side is the production choreography; the right side is the actual packet from this run, with IDs you can chase in the raw drawer.

1

Research Director + Theme Loader

Receives cycle scope and optional cross-cutting themes; hands a curriculum into Tier 0 so the seed plan is not random wandering.

theme=calendar_catalyst
2

Tier 0 Seed Router

Splits the market into entity x horizon x lens x bias x theme cells, applies validity rules by asset class, coverage penalties, topology/frontier boosts, calendar inserts, and deterministic seed entropy.

DYDX / 1w / sentiment / consensus_confirm / calendar_catalyst
3

MarketEvolve Policy

Stamps the active research-policy genome onto the seed: prompt variant, tool menu width, evidence budget, source-family targets, learned-tool preference, and experiment arm when A/B is active.

prompt_variant=flash_temporal_v4
4

Tool Atlas + Scout Harness

Ranks the approved read-only atlas for this cell, executes a tiny evidence slice, logs receipts, and lets the model request at most a bounded follow-up loop.

2 receipts / 12 exposed tools
5

Deep Scout Model

Receives the system contract plus the cell packet, evidence summaries, prior strings, and allowed tools. It returns only strict JSON.

deepseek:v4-flash
6

Normalizer + Persistor

Normalizes model JSON into hypothesis, information strings, event intelligence, node intelligence, and tool proposals. Invalid or weak fields become quality flags rather than silent truth.

scout_id=scout_3a9851b58e
7

Information Map

Stores strings as causal objects, writes evidence refs, and upserts graph nodes/edges from the entity chain and mechanism.

3 strings
8

Attention Synthesis

Reads many strings for the cycle, finds confluences and tensions, and promotes only a small set into verification budget.

synthesis_id=isyn_889ee5b95006
9

Alpha Geometry

Projects stored strings into market cells. Coordinates encode source independence, frontier pressure, tension, fragility, support, and verifier readiness.

widen_scouts
10

MarketEvolve Evaluator

Scores the research policy itself, mutates prompt/routing/tool policy, and creates a matched hard experiment instead of blindly adopting the change.

exploit_learned_tool_surface
11

Monitor + Phone Surface

Joins the persisted objects back together so the user can inspect the story, IDs, receipts, prompt, output, graph shape, and next route from one screen.

6 monitor strings

How the slice is chosen

The full swarm uses these gates to cover the market strategically. This smoke export shows one specimen cell, but the fields are the same for 1,000 scouts.

market cellDYDX / 1w / sentiment / consensus_confirm / calendar_catalyst
determinismfixed smoke seed; full swarm uses stable rng unless randomized
theme injectioncalendar_catalyst
coverage controlcoverage_cell_key + coverage_log penalty in SeedCell payload
frontier controltopology/alpha-geometry route seeds and density boost
event gatemev_c250ae020d922fe6
tool retrievallexical lens terms + BM25 atlas retrieval + source-family targets
tool budget2 evidence calls in this run; hard cap 8 per scout
policy lineagemprog_305b736b8ad24821

The live packet

These are the IDs and control fields that move through the system.

cyclecycle_groundup_node_20260521
seedseed_cycle_live_scout_canary_20260522T141613Z_T_0003
scoutscout_3a9851b58e
hypothesishyp_e89fd175f7
info stringsistr_38e1c38e6b2fd6a8, istr_eb6e0817a415bbb1, istr_e05d54b3f62c9c46
evidence refstc_0ba609789e0044bf8baf347d, tc_7db59320cd1b4d4ab2960291
routewiden_scouts
policy mutationexploit_learned_tool_surface
istr_38e1c38e6b2fd6a8istr_eb6e0817a415bbb1istr_e05d54b3f62c9c46mev_c250ae020d922fe6nint_f041ef85c44745bfatp_5bd71a9b565ea801atp_8f6c22588fb044a8atp_94b8b8b201afe9d2
10b / handoff contract

The packet changes shape at each boundary.

Each party receives a narrower or richer object, not a vague blob. That is what makes the layer inspectable and lets future agents improve it without breaking the whole system.

Seed packet

Cell plus policy becomes the scout job

DYDX / 1w / sentiment / consensus_confirm / calendar_catalyst; prompt=flash_temporal_v4; allowed_tools=12.

Evidence packet

Receipts are attached before the model call

tc_0ba609789e0044bf8baf347d, tc_7db59320cd1b4d4ab2960291

Model packet

The model emits structured JSON

hypothesis_id=hyp_e89fd175f7; confidence=0.55.

Map packet

The JSON becomes durable graph memory

strings=3, event=1, node=1, tool_proposals=3.

Route packet

Geometry says what to do next

top_cell=DYDX|1w|sentiment|calendar_catalyst; route=widen_scouts.

Evolution packet

The policy learns from the run

score=75%; mutation=exploit_learned_tool_surface; experiment=mexp_94ad691e926f7bce.

10c / data in, data out

The real layer ledger.

This is the clean version of the raw logs: every layer gets a typed input and emits a typed output. The IDs are the connective tissue.

Tier 0 seed routing

A single scout job with a stable replayable slice.

In: cycle scope, entity universe, horizons, lenses, bias modes, themes, coverage and topology state

Out: seed_id=seed_cycle_live_scout_canary_20260522T141613Z_T_0003; cell=DYDX / 1w / sentiment / consensus_confirm / calendar_catalyst

Policy application

The research genome touches the live scout before spend happens.

In: active MarketEvolve program plus any matched experiment assignment

Out: prompt_variant=flash_temporal_v4; tool_budget=12 exposed tools

Tool harness

The model receives receipts, not raw unlimited tool freedom.

In: cell, atlas rows, source-family targets, argument inference, hard evidence cap

Out: tool_call_log_ids=tc_0ba609789e0044bf8baf347d, tc_7db59320cd1b4d4ab2960291

Model contract

The model output is parseable into durable objects.

In: system prompt, user prompt, evidence receipts, prior strings, allowed tool list

Out: hypothesis=DYDX sentiment will remain neutral-to-negative over the next week as the funding spread anomaly (8.26% annualized) sign...

Persistence

Everything important gets an ID and a consumer.

In: parsed JSON, normalized strings, event/node fallbacks, quality review

Out: hypothesis=hyp_e89fd175f7; strings=3; events=1; node=1

Attention synthesis

Broad scout output is narrowed before verifier spend.

In: recent cycle strings ordered by attention score

Out: synthesis=isyn_889ee5b95006; promoted=1

Alpha geometry

The map shape becomes a routing instruction.

In: strings, evidence refs, source families, scout counts, novelty/crowdedness/conviction

Out: top_cell=DYDX|1w|sentiment|calendar_catalyst; route=widen_scouts

MarketEvolve

The research policy changes only through measured evidence.

In: cycle metrics, geometry quality, learned-tool usage, proposal backlog, hard experiment history

Out: score=75%; mutation=exploit_learned_tool_surface

Monitor

The user gets a legible surface with the raw trail one tap away.

In: database rows joined by scout_id, string IDs, event IDs, node IDs, proposal IDs

Out: strings=6; proposals=5

Where the data lands

Storage is the product architecture: strings, receipts, nodes, geometry, tools, and evolution lineage are separate surfaces with explicit consumers.

hypotheses

hyp_e89fd175f7

Scout output. Consumed by dedup, verifier, analyst, monitor.

information_strings

istr_38e1c38e6b2fd6a8, istr_eb6e0817a415bbb1, istr_e05d54b3f62c9c46

Causal strings. Consumed by synthesis, geometry, context retrieval, verifier handoff.

information_string_evidence

tc_0ba609789e0044bf8baf347d, tc_7db59320cd1b4d4ab2960291

Receipt edges. Consumed by audit, source-family scoring, verifier repair.

information_map_nodes/edges

DYDX, Hyperliquid Perp, Bybit Perp, sentiment

Entity, mechanism, and theme graph. Consumed by context retrieval and geometry.

market_event_intelligence

mev_c250ae020d922fe6

Event object plus data points/watch triggers. Consumed by scouts and monitor.

node_intelligence_snapshots

nint_f041ef85c44745bf

Hydromancer/node-native observation bundle. Consumed by map, tool proposals, monitor.

analysis_tool_proposals

atp_5bd71a9b565ea801, atp_8f6c22588fb044a8, atp_94b8b8b201afe9d2

Missing-capability requests. Consumed by learned-tool eval and runtime-adapter work orders.

information_syntheses

isyn_889ee5b95006

Confluences/tensions/promoted hypotheses. Consumed by verifier budget gate.

information_geometry_snapshots

DYDX|1w|sentiment|calendar_catalyst

Cell coordinates and route directive. Consumed by next seed plan.

market_evolve_*

meval_15afd701722d40e4, mmut_088bffe59e6d422b, mexp_94ad691e926f7bce

Program score, mutation, and hard experiment lineage.

Compact run packet

This is the complete trace in one JSON-shaped object for quick audit.

11 / geometry and evolution

The map shape decides what the system should do next.

RRGs make rotation visible. This layer does the same for research: each cell is placed by source independence and frontier pressure, lifted by tension, sized by support, colored by fragility, and converted into a route directive. The outcome is not always "trade now"; sometimes the high-signal answer is "widen scouts" or "exploit the learned node tool before spending more."

6geometry cells
67%top scream
83%verifier readiness
75%evaluator score

Alpha-geometry field

Dots higher and farther right have better frontier pressure and source independence. Larger dots have more support. Dimmer dots are more fragile.

frontier pressure
source independence
1widen scouts
2widen scouts
3widen scouts
4widen scouts
5widen scouts
6widen scouts

Top cell reading

The cell is interesting but too thin. The map is asking for more independent scouts before it becomes a trade candidate.

Xsource independence 100%
Yfrontier pressure 65%
Ztension 51%
Colorfragility 0%
Sizesupport mass 73%
Routewiden scouts
observe

No urgent route directive

The exported cells are being watched, but none crossed the verify/repair/widen threshold.

Cortex geometry controls

The evaluator now audits the shape itself: whether the field is routing enough cells, staying too quiet around clean high-signal cells, or sending fragile cells to verifier spend. Those diagnostics can mutate geometry weights and verifier thresholds, so the cortex can improve the map it is reading.

action rate100%
observe rate0%
route entropy0%
fragile verify0%
quiet high-signal0%

Cortex shape review

This is the meta layer: the cortex reads the geometry as an object, grades whether the field is mute, brittle, or executable, and emits the policy pressure that MarketEvolve can test.

routed cells3
route queue3
route action rate100%
contract success0%
fragile verify0%
quiet high-signal0%
info

shape operational

The geometry field is readable and has an executable next route; keep routing and measuring.

Geometry policy patch

The review does not silently change the system. It produces a patch, target metrics, and kill gates that have to survive matched experiments before promotion.

{
  "falsification_gates": [
    {
      "metric": "candidate_evaluator_score",
      "operator": ">",
      "reason": "If the shape is healthy, any mutation still needs to beat the active evaluator score.",
      "threshold": 0.0
    }
  ],
  "mutation_kind_hint": "continue_shape_guided_routing",
  "policy_patch": {},
  "target_metrics": {
    "geometry_route_action_rate": "increase",
    "route_contract_success_rate": "increase"
  }
}

Cortex-driven mutation proof

The smoke includes a bad-shape fixture: when route contracts are observed failing, the cortex review is allowed to become a MarketEvolve candidate mutation with its source diagnostics preserved in the proof packet.

{
  "diagnostic_codes": [
    "route_contract_not_moving_edges"
  ],
  "mutation_kind": "tighten_shape_route_contract",
  "mutation_source": {
    "diagnostic_codes": [
      "route_contract_not_moving_edges"
    ],
    "diagnostics": [
      {
        "code": "route_contract_not_moving_edges",
        "diagnosis": "Geometry-routed scouts are seeing the route but not closing the requested missing edge often enough.",
        "severity": "high",
        "target_metrics": [
          "route_contract_success_rate",
          "route_contract_failure_rate"
        ]
      }
    ],
    "falsification_gates": [
      {
        "metric": "candidate_route_contract_success_rate",
        "operator": ">=",
        "reason": "Shape-routed scouts must close or falsify the missing edge.",
        "threshold": 0.7
      }
    ],
    "schema_version": "alpha_geometry_cortex_review_v1",
    "shape_can_direct_next": true,
    "shape_health": {
      "avg_fragility": 0.0889,
      "avg_frontier_pressure": 0.6329,
      "avg_source_independence": 0.5556,
      "avg_trade_scream_score": 0.555,
      "avg_verifier_readiness": 0.8252,
      "cell_count": 3,
      "fragile_verify_rate": 0.0,
      "high_signal_observe_rate": 0.0,
      "route_action_rate": 1.0,
      "route_contract_success_rate": 0.0,
      "route_directive_counts": {
        "widen_scouts": 3
      },
      "route_entropy": 0.0,
      "routed_cell_count": 3,
      "routing_queue_length": 3
    },
    "source": "alpha_geometry_cortex_review",
    "status": "ready",
    "target_metrics": {
      "route_contract_failure_rate": "decrease",
      "route_contract_success_rate": "increase"
    },
    "work_order_ids": [
      "gcx_execute_shape_task_bb962223b377",
      "gcx_propose_geometry_policy_mutation",
      "gcx_evolution_frontier_followup"
    ]
  },
  "policy_patch": {
    "prompt_policy": {
      "route_contract_pressure": "strict"
    },
    "routing_thresholds": {
      "repair_fragility_min": 0.55,
      "route_contract_min_success_rate": 0.7,
      "tension_min": 0.45,
      "tension_readiness_min": 0.45,
      "verify_allow_fragility_max": 1.0,
      "verify_readiness_min": 0.55,
      "verify_source_independence_min": 0.0,
      "verify_trade_scream_min": 0.62,
      "widen_scouts_novelty_min": 0.35,
      "widen_sources_frontier_min": 0.5,
      "widen_sources_source_max": 0.45
    }
  },
  "proof": {
    "candidate_program_created": true,
    "diagnosis_was_observed": true,
    "experiment_plan_created": true,
    "mutation_source_preserved": true
  }
}

Production control plane

The monitor now reads the same shared control payload as the swarm manifest. This is the operator-facing proof that the shape, cortex review, and evolution frontier can be inspected from the persisted run instead of only from smoke-only artifacts.

{
  "diagnostic_codes": [
    "shape_operational"
  ],
  "lineage_frontier_count": 4,
  "mutation_kind_hint": "continue_shape_guided_routing",
  "policy_patch_present": false,
  "schema_version": "swarm_evolution_control_v1",
  "shape_can_direct_next": true,
  "source": "monitor_live_rebuild"
}

Dispatchable cortex tasks

The control plane is now writable at the right point in the cycle: route and cortex work orders become task contracts with allowed tools, success gates, stop conditions, budgets, and blackboard events. Re-running the dispatch is idempotent, so the cortex can keep inspecting without flooding the queue.

{
  "existing_count": 0,
  "posted_count": 7,
  "schema_version": "evolution_control_task_dispatch_v1",
  "status": "posted",
  "tasks": [
    {
      "allowed_tools": [
        "tic://tool/talis_native/plan_alpha_geometry_actions@v1",
        "tic://tool/talis_native/review_alpha_geometry_cortex@v1"
      ],
      "id": "task_98bb38eab982",
      "market_evolve_cortex_policy": null,
      "market_evolve_experiment_arm": null,
      "market_evolve_experiment_id": null,
      "market_evolve_program_id": null,
      "stop_condition": "frontier action either produces matched evidence or an explicit rejected proof packet",
      "success_gate": "frontier action either produces matched evidence or an explicit rejected proof packet",
      "title": "market_evolve: mutate active policy :: mprog_3b7ef2f2787c7251",
      "topic": "market_evolve.frontier"
    },
    {
      "allowed_tools": [
        "tic://tool/talis_native/plan_alpha_geometry_actions@v1",

      "id": "task_c6e7ff44c0c9",

      "market_evolve_experiment_arm": null,
      "market_evolve_experiment_id": null,
      "market_evolve_program_id": null,
      "stop_condition": "Stop when an independent scout confirms, contradicts, or kills the original cell.",
      "success_gate": "second scout confirms, contradicts, or kills the string",
      "title": "seed_router: replicate with independent scouts :: validator_unstake",
      "topic": "alpha_geometry.route"
    }
  ]
}

Cortex worker execution

The next layer claims those contracts, starts them, reads the shape through the approved harness, then spends a bounded follow-up budget only after the shape read succeeds. In this run, the worker logged 12 follow-up observations and kept every observation tied to task events.

Evaluator feedback from worker outcomes

The worker loop now feeds MarketEvolve directly. Completion rate, task failures, pending work, shape-observation rate, bounded follow-up execution, follow-up observation yield, and shape-blocked follow-up penalties become objective terms, so the system can evolve the harness instead of merely logging that it ran.

Cortex A/B proof

The latest proof pass does not score cortex policy from one global soup. The dispatch layer creates control and candidate task clones, the worker applies each task-level cortex policy, and the evaluator reads each arm separately before MarketEvolve can promote a harness change.

experimentmexp_10f8096703657e70
control programmprog_3b7ef2f2787c7251
candidate programmprog_6b6dc53a438f3428
control tasks1.0000
candidate tasks1.0000
control follow-up rate100%
candidate follow-up rate100%
candidate observations/task1.0000

Cortex work order

The shape is converted into ranked tasks with an owner, first tool, follow-up tool sequence, missing edges, success gate, stop condition, and map-update rule. This is the part that lets the cortex look at the field and immediately know where to direct workers next.

next route tasknone
primary ownercortex
primary actionobserve
queue length6
rank 1 / seed_router

replicate with independent scouts

The cell is novel but still needs independent scout replication.

{
  "missing_edges": [
    "frontier_cell -> next_receptive_field",
    "single_scout_cell -> independent_replication"
  ],
  "must_call_first": null,
  "route_directive": "widen_scouts",
  "route_task_id": "shape_task_eb71540bad8f",
  "success_gate": "second scout confirms, contradicts, or kills the string",
  "tool_sequence": null
}
rank 2 / seed_router

rank 3 / seed_router

replicate with independent scouts

The cell is novel but still needs independent scout replication.

{
  "missing_edges": [
    "frontier_cell -> next_receptive_field",
    "single_scout_cell -> independent_replication"
  ],
  "must_call_first": null,
  "route_directive": "widen_scouts",
  "route_task_id": "shape_task_aa452321c1f3",
  "success_gate": "second scout confirms, contradicts, or kills the string",
  "tool_sequence": null
}

Shape-supervision work orders

These are the orders the cortex would hand to router, verifier, tool builder, or MarketEvolve after reviewing the map shape.

[
  {
    "action": "replicate_with_independent_scouts",
    "order_id": "gcx_execute_shape_task_bb962223b377",
    "owner": "seed_router",
    "route_task_id": "shape_task_bb962223b377",
    "success_gate": "second scout confirms, contradicts, or kills the string"
  },
  {
    "action": "mutate_active_policy",
    "order_id": "gcx_evolution_frontier_followup",
    "owner": "market_evolve",
    "route_task_id": null,
    "success_gate": "frontier action either produces matched evidence or an explicit rejected proof packet"
  }
]

Cortex toolkit

The first tool is the native shape reader. Other tools are pulled from the action packet so the next worker can inspect, move, or falsify the edge instead of improvising.

11b / evaluator-guided improvement

The system then runs a small evolution arena.

MarketEvolve treats the run as evidence about the research program itself. It scores the current policy, proposes a bounded set of diverse candidate mutations, and creates matched A/B experiments so a child has to beat control out of sample before it becomes active.

Evaluator

Score 75%, gates passed

score=0.75; strings=6; valid=1.00; src_ind=0.56; ready=0.83; fragility=0.09; governor_seeds=0; governor_yield=0.00; cortex_tasks=0; cortex_done=0.00; flags=hard_gates_passed

First candidate

exploit learned tool surface

Learned tools are producing successful evidence calls; bias future scout tool surfaces toward them.

Hard experiment

matched_policy_ab

Assign seed_cell by paired_seed_clone_then_deterministic_hash; primary metric: accepted_unique_high_quality_coverage_per_dollar; min seeds per arm: 20.

Candidate proof contracts

Each child is born with a reason, a policy patch, target metrics, a population gate, promotion requirements, and explicit kill signals. This is the difference between "try a prompt" and a real evolving research program.

candidate 1 / proposed

exploit learned tool surface

If proven learned tools are exposed earlier, scouts gather more useful evidence without widening raw call count.

sourcemarket evolve metric heuristic
candidate rank0
population gateallowed
open siblings0/4
kill gates3
target metrics3

Would change

  • tool_request_policy.prefer_learned_toolsFalse -> True
  • tool_request_policy.learned_tool_priority_boost0.0000 -> 1.0000
  • tool_request_policy.max_tool_candidates_per_seed10 -> 11

What kills it

  • score_delta < 0.0500reject_or_continue_candidate
  • candidate_valid_string_rate < 1.0000reject_candidate
  • candidate_tool_activation_rate <= 0.2000reject_candidate
{
  "child_program_id": "mprog_3b7ef2f2787c7251",
  "hypothesis": "If proven learned tools are exposed earlier, scouts gather more useful evidence without widening raw call count.",
  "intended_effect": "Learned tools are producing successful evidence calls; bias future scout tool surfaces toward them.",
  "mutation_id": "mmut_088bffe59e6d422b",
  "parent_program_id": "mprog_305b736b8ad24821",
  "promotion_evidence_required": [
    "matched control/candidate seed slices",
    "multi-cycle out-of-sample candidate win",
    "hard gates passed",
    "no cost explosion",
    "lineage remains human-readable"
  ],
  "target_metrics": {
    "learned_tool_success_rate": 1.0,
    "learned_tool_usage_per_scout": 1.0,
    "tool_activation_rate": 0.2
  }
}

Evolution proof packet

Every candidate mutation must name what it saw in the shape, what changed in the cortex policy, which metrics should move, and what evidence would kill the candidate. That is the AlphaEvolve-style loop: propose, test against control, promote only if the evidence survives.

{
  "changed_paths": [
    {
      "after": true,
      "before": false,
      "path": "tool_request_policy.prefer_learned_tools"
    },
    {
      "after": 1.0,
      "before": 0.0,
      "path": "tool_request_policy.learned_tool_priority_boost"
    },
    {
      "after": 11,
      "before": 10,
      "path": "tool_request_policy.max_tool_candidates_per_seed"
    }
  ],
  "falsification_gates": [
    {
      "decision": "reject_or_continue_candidate",
      "metric": "score_delta",
      "operator": "<",
      "threshold": 0.05
    },
    {
      "decision": "reject_candidate",
      "metric": "candidate_valid_string_rate",
      "operator": "<",
      "threshold": 1.0
    },
    {
      "decision": "reject_candidate",
      "metric": "candidate_tool_activation_rate",
      "operator": "<=",
      "threshold": 0.2
    }
  ],
  "hypothesis": "If proven learned tools are exposed earlier, scouts gather more useful evidence without widening raw call count.",
  "kill_signal": "Candidate does not improve active/evaluated tool usage or creates more low-EV/tool-failure backlog.",
  "mutation_kind": "exploit_learned_tool_surface",
  "schema_version": "market_evolve_mutation_proof_v1",
  "target_metrics": {
    "learned_tool_success_rate": 1.0,
    "learned_tool_usage_per_scout": 1.0,
    "tool_activation_rate": 0.2
  }
}

Hard experiment verdict

The proof packet is now executable: falsification gates are evaluated on the candidate-vs-control result and persisted with the experiment outcome. Passing the viewer means more than storing an intent; the candidate had to survive its own kill criteria.

{
  "experiment_id": "mexp_94ad691e926f7bce",
  "falsification_gate_results": [
    {
      "decision": "reject_or_continue_candidate",
      "metric": "score_delta",
      "observed": 0.6154,
      "operator": "<",
      "status": "passed",
      "threshold": 0.05,
      "triggered": false
    },
    {
      "decision": "reject_candidate",
      "metric": "candidate_valid_string_rate",
      "observed": 1.0,
      "operator": "<",
      "status": "passed",
      "threshold": 1.0,
      "triggered": false
    },
    {
      "decision": "reject_candidate",
      "metric": "candidate_tool_activation_rate",
      "observed": 1.0,
      "operator": "<=",
      "status": "passed",
      "threshold": 0.2,
      "triggered": false
    }
  ],
  "final_decision": "promote_candidate",
  "final_score_delta": 0.6154,
  "quality_flags": [
    "candidate_passed_promotion_gates",
    "candidate_wins:2",
    "completed_cycles:2",
    "consecutive_candidate_wins:2",
    "falsification_gates_evaluated:3",
    "market_evolve_online_experiment_v1",
    "multi_cycle_promotion_gate_passed",
    "proof_gate_passed:candidate_tool_activation_rate",
    "proof_gate_passed:candidate_valid_string_rate",
    "proof_gate_passed:score_delta"
  ]
}

Evolution lineage

The system now exports the ancestry graph: programs, mutation edges, proof-gate verdicts, diversity signatures, and the frontier queue that tells the cortex which policy family should mutate or continue next.

mutation edges3
frontier items3

12 / final results

What actually came out of the run.

This run passed the ground-up system smoke and produced a routed research object. It did not declare a final trade; geometry routed the cell to widen scouts.

16/16system layers passed
5/18data surfaces touched
6monitor strings
widen scoutstop route
Scout conclusion

DYDX sentiment will remain neutral-to-negative over the next week as the funding spread anomaly (8.26% annualized) signals persistent short...

Confidence 55%; kill signal: Funding spread drops below 3% annualized and price breaks above $0.66 on increasing volume, contradicting short-bias thesis.

Information string

Funding spread anomaly signals persistent short bias

Mechanism: Persistent short funding cost suggests market participants are willing to pay to hold shorts, implying bearish conviction that overrides transient upgrade news.

13 / audit drawer

The beautiful layer stays traceable.

The phone view should feel calm and designed. Underneath, every claim still has receipts. The raw system prompt, user prompt, evidence, model output, persisted row, and monitor payload remain one tap away.

Open raw prompt and output audit trail

System Prompt

live_scout_system_prompt.md | 3367 bytes
You are a Talis Flash scout in a scale repair arm. Study only the assigned market cell: entity, horizon, lens, bias, evidence, prior strings, and allowed tools in the user packet.

Goal: emit exactly one top-level hypothesis that summarizes the strongest valid information_string, plus 1-2 decision-changing information_strings. Abstain only when every provided evidence packet is empty, stale for this horizon, or unsupported by the allowed sources.

Scale-repair rules:
- Do not leave `hypothesis` empty when `information_strings` is non-empty.
- Do not leave `information_strings` empty when at least one evidence packet is fresh enough to update a watchlist, verifier task, route decision, or gap repair.
- If the evidence is thin, write a low-conviction map string with a concrete missing-edge kill_signal rather than vague prose.
- Every string must include time_horizon, time_scale, observed_at, source_time_basis, expires_at, temporal_confidence, extends_or_contradicts, and would_change_decision=true.
- Every evidence_refs value must cite a provided tool_call_log_id or source ref. If none exists, abstain and say the missing source in rationale_brief.
- suggested_tools must be copied exactly from allowed_tool_candidates; if none are useful, return an empty list rather than inventing.
- No prose outside JSON. Keep JSON small.

Return strict JSON only:
{
  "hypothesis": "<falsifiable one-sentence claim, or empty string to abstain>",
  "confidence": 0.0,
  "rationale_brief": "<mechanism, max 180 chars>",
  "suggested_tools": ["<copy tic://... from allowed_tool_candidates>", "..."],
  "information_strings": [
    {
      "title": "<short title>",
      "thesis": "<entity -> mechanism -> market implication>",
      "entities_chain": ["<entity>", "<actor/venue/theme>"],
      "mechanism": "<why this should reprice or update the map>",
      "depth_layers": [{"layer": 1, "claim": "<direct effect>"}, {"layer": 2, "claim": "<second-order effect>"}],
      "expected_outcome": "<observable confirmation>",
      "time_horizon": "<tick|minute|hour|intraday|1d|1w|1m|structural>",
      "time_scale": "<same grain as claim>",
      "observed_at": "<ISO8601 source/tool observation time, or as_of_utc>",
      "source_time_basis": "<event_time|ingestion_time|publication_time|valid_time|unknown>",
      "kill_signal": "<what breaks the chain>",
      "extends_or_contradicts": "<new|extends|contradicts|abandons>",
      "would_change_decision": true,
      "expires_at": "<when stale>",
      "crowdedness": 0.0,
      "conviction": 0.0,
      "novelty_score": 0.0,
      "evidence_refs": ["<tool_call_log_id/source ref>"],
      "temporal_confidence": 0.0
    }
  ]
}

<market_evolve_prompt_contract>
contract_pressure: raise
minimum_information_strings: 2
This is an evolved policy requirement, not commentary. Before returning, self-check the JSON against it.
- Return at least 2 valid information_strings unless the cell is genuinely empty.
- Reject your own string if mechanism is missing or only narrative.
- Reject your own string if kill_signal is missing or not observable.
- Prefer evidence_refs/source refs from provided tool evidence; mark uncertainty instead of inventing refs.
- If you cannot satisfy this contract, return empty hypothesis/information_strings and explain the data gap in rationale_brief.
</market_evolve_prompt_contract>

User Prompt

live_scout_user_prompt.md | 2341 bytes
as_of_utc=2026-05-22T14:17:49.166795+00:00
Freshness rule: for intraday/1d/1w horizons, do not use old historical dates as live catalyst evidence unless explicitly labeled as regime context.

Cell:
  entity=DYDX
  horizon=1w
  lens=sentiment
  bias_mode=consensus_confirm
  theme=calendar_catalyst

          "text": "# News Details\n## Angelini Pharma to Acquire Catalyst Pharmaceuticals for 4.1 Billion USD (3.5 Billion Euros), Entering the U.S. Market and Consolidating its Leadership in Brain Health and Rare Disease\nFounded in 2002 and listed on Nasdaq since 2006, Catalyst has built a robust portfolio of products focused on the treatment of rare neuromuscular and neurological diseases: FIRDAPSE \u00ae (amifampridine), the only evidence-based treatment approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for Lambert-Eaton myasthenic syndrome (LEMS) in patients aged six and above; AGAMREE \u00ae (vamorolone) a novel corticosteroid approved by the FDA in 2023 for the treatment of Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy (DMD) in patients aged 2 and above; and **FYCOMPA** \u00ae (perampanel), an antiepileptic drug approved for the ",

            "source": null,
            "text": "# News Details\n## Angelini Pharma to Acquire Catalyst Pharmaceuticals for 4.1 Billion USD (3.5 Billion Euros), Entering the U.S. Market and Consolidating its Leadership in Brain Health and Rare Disease\nFounded in 2002 and listed on Nasdaq since 2006, Catalyst has built a robust portfolio of products focused on the treatment of rare neuromuscular and neurological diseases: FIRDAPSE \u00ae (amifampridine), the only evidence-based treatment approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for Lambert-Eaton myasthenic syndrome (LEMS) in patients aged six and above; AGAMREE \u00ae (vamorolone) a novel corticosteroid approved by the FDA in 2023 for the treatment of Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy (DMD) in patients aged 2 and above; and **FYCOMPA** \u00ae (perampanel), an antiepileptic drug approved for the ",

        {

          ],
          "key": "order_flow_sim",
          "source_family": "order_flow",
          "status": "available",
          "title": "Order-flow and simulation"
        }
      ]
    },
    "routing_overlays": [
      {
        "effect": "recently covered cells with no downstream publish are downweighted",
        "name": "coverage penalty",
        "source": "coverage_log"
      },
      {
        "effect": "sparse/frontier regions are oversampled, dense consensus regions are underweighted",
        "name": "topology frontier boost",
        "source": "topology_density_map"
      },
      {
        "effect": "verify, repair, resolve-tension, widen-source, and widen-scout cells get explicit next-cycle scouts",
        "name": "alpha-geometry route seeds",
        "source": "information_geometry_snapshots from the prior cycle"
      },
      {
        "effect": "must-research event cells are prepended before the normal random budget",
        "name": "calendar gate",
        "source": "calendar_gate / catalyst clock"
      },
      {
        "effect": "each seed receives lexical candidates plus dynamic tool/source matches for its query text",
        "name": "BM25 tool retrieval",
        "source": "tool_atlas"
      }
    ],
    "sampling_policy": {
      "base_sampler": "Latin hypercube over valid entity x horizon x lens x bias cells",
      "default_seed_budget": 1000,
      "determinism": "rng_seed can make the slice replayable; otherwise cycle/scope time seeds the generator",
      "stratified_budget": "900 broad-market scouts before calendar/geometry injections",
      "theme_injection": "100 dedicated theme scouts when themes exist"
    },
    "status": "available",
    "validity": {
      "rule": "asset-class gates remove nonsensical lenses before sampling",
      "valid_cell_count": 132048,
      "valid_counts_by_asset_class": {
        "equity": 13104,
        "hyperliquid_perp": 115920,
        "index_macro": 3024
      }
    }
  },

Study only the assigned market cell: entity, horizon, lens, bias, evidence, prior strings, and allowed tools in the user packet.\n\nGoal: emit exactly one top-level hypothesis that summarizes the strongest valid information_string, plus 1-2 decision-changing information_strings. Abstain only when every provided evidence packet is empty, stale for this horizon, or unsupported by the allowed sources.\n\nScale-repair rules:\n- Do not leave `hypothesis` empty when `information_strings` is non-empty.\n- Do not leave `information_strings` empty when at least one evidence packet is fresh enough to update a watchlist, verifier task, route decision, or gap repair.\n- If the evidence is thin, write a low-conviction map string with a concrete missing-edge kill_signal rather than vague prose.\n- Every string must include time_horizon, time_scale, observed_at, source_time_basis, expires_at, temporal_confidence, extends_or_contradicts, and would_change_decision=true.\n- Every evidence_refs value must cite a provided tool_call_log_id or source ref. Keep JSON small.\n\nReturn strict JSON only:\n{\n  \"hypothesis\": \"<falsifiable one-sentence claim, or empty string to abstain>\",\n  \"confidence\": 0.0,\n  \"rationale_brief\": \"<mechanism, max 180 chars>\",\n  \"suggested_tools\": [\"<copy tic://... from allowed_tool_candidates>\", \"...\"],\n  \"information_strings\": [\n    {\n      \"title\": \"<short title>\",\n      \"thesis\": \"<entity -> mechanism -> market implication>\",\n      \"entities_chain\": [\"<entity>\", \"<actor/venue/theme>\"],\n      \"mechanism\": \"<why this should reprice or update the map>\",\n      \"depth_layers\": [{\"layer\": 1, \"claim\": \"<direct effect>\"}, {\"layer\": 2, \"claim\": \"<second-order effect>\"}],\n      \"expected_outcome\": \"<observable confirmation>\",\n      \"time_horizon\": \"<tick|minute|hour|intraday|1d|1w|1m|structural>\",\n      \"time_scale\": \"<same grain as claim>\",\n      \"observed_at\": \"<ISO8601 source/tool observation time, or as_of_utc>\",\n      \"source_time_basis\": \"<event_time|ingestion_time|publication_time|valid_time|unknown>\",\n      \"kill_signal\": \"<what breaks the chain>\",\n      \"extends_or_contradicts\": \"<new|extends|contradicts|abandons>\",\n      \"would_change_decision\": true,\n      \"expires_at\": \"<when stale>\",\n      \"crowdedness\": 0.0,\n      \"conviction\": 0.0,\n      \"novelty_score\": 0.0,\n      \"evidence_refs\": [\"<tool_call_log_id/source ref>\"],\n      \"temporal_confidence\": 0.0\n    }\n  ]\n}\n\n<market_evolve_prompt_contract>\ncontract_pressure: raise\nminimum_information_strings: 2\nThis is an evolved policy requirement, not commentary. Before returning, self-check the JSON against it.\n- Return at least 2 valid information_strings unless the cell is genuinely empty.\n- Reject your own string if mechanism is missing or only narrative.\n- Reject your own string if kill_signal is missing or not observable.\n- Prefer evidence_refs/source refs from provided tool evidence; mark uncertainty instead of inventing refs.\n- If you cannot satisfy this contract, return empty hypothesis/information_strings and explain the data gap in rationale_brief.\n</market_evolve_prompt_contract>",
